review_text:

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
A clean, tidy & quiet corner with a relax community atmosphere. The oversize sculpture, Iggy's Pod hanging over the vast atrium is a sight to behold.
There are onsite recycle facilities for electronic  (computers, printers), batteries  and lightbulbs (some LED lightbulbs contains mercury, so please dispose off responsibly).  Facility to replace your Trace Together Token too!
A short walk round the corner is Pet Corner, colourful birds & bird's songs welcome you. There are several coffee shops all within walking distance which cater for breakfast, lunch and dinner.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Facilities are limited, but serves their purposes.
The one I know about are the study room and gym.
Take the lift beside the counter to go to the amenities.
Study room has no wifi, and just some old office tables for you to do your own things. Water cooler is just outside.
Gym is on level 3, with limited equipment.
There is a free weights rack for squats, deadlifts, bench press.
There are free weight dumbbells, resistance machines, treadmills and inclined bench.
Quite a small cozy gym with not many people. Closes at 930pm. See rates and packages in pictures.
Note that non-members who didn't purchase the package cannot come during certain timings.
They can only come when the counter opens, which closes at 6pm.
Go to the counter to exchange for a pass to go to the gym. You need to return the pass afterwards.
Be sure to call in if you are unsure so as to not make a wasted trip!

Like all CCs, they also offer all kinds of services like singpass reset, or classes to sign up for! Check with them for further details.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

review_text: The park itself is fine if you don't intend to go onto the other side of the waterway because the number of bridges linking the two sides are few and far apart. I am not sure why it is designed like that. We happened to be on the opposite side of the "main attractions" like Waterway Point, SAFRA, etc and we just could not get over easily. I guess if you are aiming to have lots of steps, this is the place to be.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We enrolled our daughter to MindChamps Buangkok earlier this year and it has been a great decision for us.

The teachers are caring and take care of our daughter well. Kudos to outgoing principal Ms Shannon Lim, who has really done so much for the school and laid out what works. We are sure the foundations that you’ve laid will help the next principal to take the role like a duck to water, and build on the good work you’ve done.

The year end concert was so well thought through, from the theme itself, to the art exhibitions that the children have done and extending to the actual year end performance. Such wonderful attention to detail!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: We had a wonderful time at the family carnival! The teachers were very friendly and supportive, which made the event a great success. The kids especially enjoyed the activities, with the bouncy castle being a big highlight.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We are so grateful to have found MindChamps Preschool @ Buangkok! From the very first day, the warmth, professionalism, and dedication of the team have been outstanding. The teachers are nurturing and genuinely committed to the children’s growth, creating a safe and stimulating environment where every child feels valued and encouraged to explore.

A special mention goes to Principal Shannon, whose leadership truly shines through in the centre. She is approachable, attentive, and deeply invested in both the children’s well-being and the parents’ partnership with the school. Shannon always takes the time to listen, provide thoughtful guidance, and ensure that the centre maintains the highest standards of care and education. Her passion and positivity are evident in every interaction, and it sets the tone for the entire school community.

We’ve seen such wonderful progress in our child’s confidence, language, and love of learning since joining. MindChamps @ Buangkok feels like a second home, and we couldn’t be happier with our experience here. Highly recommend this centre to any parent looking for a nurturing and inspiring preschool journey!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
The teachers, supporting staff, and principal at MindChamps Buangkok are very attentive to my son’s development. They provide constructive feedback and guidance, which has helped him improve and grow tremendously.
We also appreciate the well-rounded curriculum that balances academics with play, creativity, and social skills. One of the key highlights for us is the bilingual emphasis, especially the English phonics program and the Chinese curriculum, which have both been engaging and effective in building a strong foundation.
We are truly grateful for the dedication and care shown by Ms Shannon and her team, and would highly recommend MindChamps Buangkok any time!

review_text:
We are grateful for the wonderful care and support my girl received during her time at MindChamps.

The teachers created a nurturing environment that helped her grow in confidence and independence, while learning important values like kindness and teamwork.

We also truly enjoyed celebrating Chinese New Year together with her and the school — it was a very special memory for our family.

Thank you for being such a meaningful part of her early years ❤️

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Quiet, underrated gem in the northeast. Punggol Park has a really chill vibe, especially around the big lake in the middle. Great for evening walks, light jogs, or just sitting down to unwind after a long day.

Nice mix of facilities too. There’s a Tenderbest nearby for easy bites, a gym pod if you want a quick workout, plus water coolers and vending machines around for convenience. You’ll also spot exercise stations catered for the elderly, which is a thoughtful touch. There is a nice woodball corner as well and also an open area for football.

Plenty of shaded paths and open spaces, so it never feels too crowded. You’ll see families, cyclists, and even people fishing by the pond. Simple, not overly fancy, but that’s part of the charm.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
If u are in Hougang / Sengkang area, and wanted a park to stroll or exercise.  Try this Punggol Park.
Pls note that although it called Punggol Park, it’s actually in end of upper Serangoon, Hougang area.
It’s a smaller to Bedok Reservior Park, but it’s wide enough for most outdoor exercise.
Peaceful and tranquil.  U can have a drink/ food at the eatery: Tenderbest.  They have both indoor AC and alfresco dining area.

Also, if u want to walk/ cycle more, it’s just 5mins walk away to the Serangoon Park Connector.  Which will lead u to Kallang southwards or Northwards will lead u to Punggol Point or Changi Pt via Pasir Ris PCN.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Actually, It's a Nice and quiet park with a running loop around a pond.The park with a lot of place to rest chair benches. It's shady with huge trees and bird chirping. The place is Great for walking, jogging or biking. You can come here anytime u want some peace and to de- stress through a nice and scenic walk in the park. At Night time the lights around the pond makes for a cool background. Overall a good place to bring the family and have some exercise. 🌳🌱🌿🍂🍃🍀🪺🌸💮🐦🐦‍⬛🚴🏃🚶🧎🤸

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: attended a 3 day wake at the oratory of the Church of Nativity recently. On one of the evenings, I went for a walk in the park. It was pleasant to be in nature, to reflect on the life. The park was however crowded in the evening with people exercising, cycling. walking their dogs and having picnics. Love the trees.

review_text: Visited this community club for a swim on a Tuesday and it was a really pleasant experience. The crowd was minimal, which made the whole session feel relaxed and unhurried. There are two pools available—one indoor and one outdoor—so you can choose depending on your preference or weather. There’s also a feature pool that has just been renovated and recently reopened, and it looks clean and refreshed. Overall, it’s a nice, well-maintained spot for a quiet swim without the usual crowd. There are lifeguards on duty to keep us safe!

review_text:

2 large full length swimming pool. One of the pool is under shelter.
Locker is available for valuable item storage.
Parking is available for car, motocycle,  Bicycle.
Next to food court and there are food available together at the sport complex.
There will be swimming classes and may be crowded some of the time.
Limited table and chair around the pool while there is a stand next to the open pool.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
This swimming pool finally reopened after more than 3 years of closure.

It now has 5 types of slide rides, with Rainbow dash being the only multi-slide ride. They are all MUCH milder than the ones in Wild Wild Wet.

The lifeguards are pretty strict about height limits:

Purple slide (Cotton Candy Bowl) - 1.3m height limit

orange slide (Sugar Rush) - 1.3m height limit

Green slide (Jelly Lime Loops) - 1.2m height limit

Pink slide (Jellybears Swiggle) - 1.2m height limit

The lifeguards are also very particular about wearing of "hard objects", even watches by adults/parents. Yet, ironically, I see authorized influencers being allowed to wear GoPro cameras!!

There are mini paddle boats for children 4yo and above, with minimum weight limit 30 kg, and requires adult supervision at all times.

Water guns are NOT Allowed !!

Toilets are clean.

There's no more food eatery at the balcony area near the entrance.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
The swimming complex is quite big and has 4 adult water slides. Note that jewellery such as earrings or necklaces are not allowed, and swimsuits with zippers are prohibited. Kids need to be at least 120–130 cm tall to use the slides.

It gets very crowded on weekends, especially at the kids’ water play area. The adult slides were fine, just some queuing, especially for the longer slide. The Sugar Rush slide was really fast and fun.

The biggest downside is the changing/shower rooms. Around 5:30 PM, the queue gets extremely long, so it’s better to go earlier if you want to avoid the wait.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: It was a super bad experience at the sliding pool. My two girls was not allowed to take the slide just because their swimming wear got a zipper at the front. Going down slides shld be by sitting and sliding down. How in the world the front zipper affect the slide down process. Really disappointed with the system enforced. Really disappoint the kids on their school holiday.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Located in the middle of Serangoon, it is highly accessible and connects both the NEL and Circle line within a stop.

The area in front of the gantries is spacious and more than sufficient for crowds to pass through despite groups of people with low situation awareness blocking key passageways, especially during peak hours.

There are a few convenient shops underground, and were lifesavers during the pandemic, selling single pieces of masks at non-cutthroat prices for those who were forgetful enough to leave house without one.

The many ATMs here make this area a great dealing spot for Carousell users, but make sure the person you are dealing with is on the same side of the gantry as you, for obvious reasons.

Beware of the overzealous staff standing slightly behind the gantry, who would conduct random bag checks the moment you make eye contact. Just walk and move on with life.

review_text: Modern large bus depot for maintenance of SBS Transit trains. Reachable by two LRT stations Tongkang and Rebjong. Plenty of fasciitis including canteen and groceries. Conveniently located in the neighbourhood hartland two LRT stations to Sengkang MRT station and bus interchange Compass One Kopitium Square with plenty of dinning shopping supermarket banking library postage community centre cinemas entertainment and amenities nearby. Friendly cosy comfy welcoming ambience for groups expats locals visitors and families.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Frequently come here for my runs together with my friend.

There’s a fitness corner, running track, a soccer field and a swimming pool. There’s also a place to play basketball.

Come in the morning on weekdays if you like the whole stadium to yourself. Usually quite crowded on weekends and Friday nights.

Also, avoid coming to this stadium when it’s raining or after a rain. The running track would be semi-flooded due to poor uneven track flooring. This stadium is relatively old after all.

review_text: Staff is just ignoring you. Asked for a menu at the bar two times - the lady said “give me a minute” and never came back. And it’s not because they’re super busy - they just don’t care. This is simply disrespectful

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Restaurant have several hit and miss cuisines but the most recent visit had me trying the fish and fries. It was really good and affordable. The fish was not overly battered and there were thick flakes of fish within that is light and fluffy!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Singapore Swimming Club has a great selection of Indian food and have always enjoyed the food, especially someone who is very discerning. Having said that, off late the quality of paneer dishes seems to be off. The paneer tikka is no longer soft, creamy and flavourful. I think they may have changed their supplier or switched to using frozen paneer. The management should look into it and bring back the original quality paneer.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Been coming here for years since I’ve been a kid. Always love how homely and family-friendly this place is. The eateries are fantastic, especially the cafe and Chinese restaurant. The club sports facilities are excellent, I’ve used the pool, tennis courts, bowling alley, and gym before. With the Katong Park station almost ready nearby, it will become even more accessible.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Went there for the second time as my aunt is their member. The first round tried their lunch buffet. Decent taste in many varieties. This round tried their fried hokkien mee. The taste was perfect, the portion was big. But wait for my local coffee for a long time. This really needs to improve

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Dear Management Team,
I am writing to share a truly exceptional experience I recently had at your store.
While shopping for meat at GIANT Food, I was warmly welcomed by an outstanding employee whose name tag identified him as Mr. Youn Sang. From the moment I approached the counter, he made my entire experience feel special and valued.
What impressed me most was his attentiveness to everyone around him. No customer in line was left unattended. Mothers with children were acknowledged with patience and kindness, and every single person received a warm, personalized greeting. His professionalism, positive energy, and genuine care for customers truly stood out.
It is rare to encounter someone who takes such pride in delivering exceptional service. Mr. Youn Sang made my shopping experience not only pleasant but memorable, and I felt it was important that your management team be made aware of the remarkable job he is doing.
Please extend my sincere appreciation to him. Employees like Mr. Youn Sang are a tremendous asset to your organization.
Sincerely,
Jay.C

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Worst hypermarket experience. Only three checkout counters were open on a Saturday night, which naturally led to long queues. We waited about 40 minutes just to check out. If the store is short-staffed, it should reconsider operating 24 hours. Giant, please learn from Sheng Siong.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This is one of the best mega warehouses in Singapore to shop on the East side as it carries many brands of food, household n electrical items . The layout is good where one can shop in comfort. There is a food court that has a nice range of food where one can indulge after the shopping. A great place to stock up essentials for home as the ample parking is convenient to trolley ones purchase to the car. One can find wonderful bargains as the store holds promotional product brands that are a steal. Happy shopping , happy family.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Today, January 12, 2026, at the Giant food court, I ordered *mee hoon* soto from the Nasi Padang stall, asking for an extra chicken add-on for $2.00. After I collected the food, it was not hot and contained only about one tablespoon of chicken. I asked the lady, who sounded Indonesian, about it. She was not happy when I asked her if the $2.00 chicken had been added. She pulled the bowl, and not wanting to create a scene, I just pulled it back and left. I'm very disappointed with her attitude; at the very least, she could have explained nicely. This will be the last time.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Final Giant Hypemarket at Tampines ✔️✔️✔️✔️✔️👍👍👍

Giant Hypermarket at Tampines is ideal for one-stop grocery needs if you live in this area. There is a wide range of electronics, kitchenware, groceries, a semi-wet market, fruits, and vegetables. The prices are reasonable. There are also food stalls serving plenty of local dishes. Halal and vegetarian options are available and typically served until around 9:30 PM. Additionally, there is ample parking space with free parking available. If you live in this area, it’s an excellent place to shop for your daily needs.
Chris
17 Sept 2025 11am

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
AMK Hub is one of the most convenient and practical malls in Ang Mo Kio. The location is excellent, as it is directly connected to Ang Mo Kio MRT station and the bus interchange, making it very easy to reach by public transport.

The mall has a good mix of shops, dining options, supermarkets, daily essentials, and services. It is suitable for families, students, office workers, and residents living nearby. Whether you are looking for groceries, a quick meal, shopping, banking services, or just a place to walk around, AMK Hub has almost everything you need in one location.

I like that the mall is very accessible and easy to navigate. There are many food choices, from fast food and takeaway options to cafés and restaurants for proper meals. It is also a convenient place to run errands, meet friends, or spend time with family.

Although the mall can get quite crowded during weekends and meal times, the overall environment is still lively, clean, and well maintained. The air-conditioning is comfortable, and the shops are arranged in a way that makes it easy to move around.

Overall, AMK Hub is a reliable and convenient neighbourhood mall with a good variety of food, shopping, and essential services. It is definitely one of the best places to visit in Ang Mo Kio for everyday needs, meals, and casual shopping.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: AMK Hub is a really convenient neighbourhood mall, especially since it’s directly connected to Ang Mo Kio MRT and the bus interchange. It’s super easy to get to, which makes it a popular stop for quick meals or shopping. There’s a wide range of food options here—from food courts with local and international dishes to plenty of fast-food and restaurant chains. It’s not as exciting as the bigger malls, but it’s great for everyday needs and casual dining.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A good, classic neighbourhood mall with food options that leave you spoilt for choice. A rectangular-shaped mall with basements mostly eateries, Level 1 & 2 mostly shops, and Level 3 is mostly educational centres.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Went to AMK Hub for lunch. ate at that Malaysia Boleh. There are many varieties of food here suitable for your lunch or dinner...My hubby always eat the cray pot rice which comes with chicken and salted fish. That day we ate that fish ball black noodles. This store add some tasty dark sauce to their noodles which gives a different taste to it's noodles. Taste very yummy. I had their fried tang hoon. Same store...They have varies type of fried noodles which you must tried. Not bad too. We always end our meal with a bowl of Chendol which is classic with all Malaysia Boleh outlets...They definitely have this menu in all their outlets without fail...AMK Hub is filled with varieties type of food on their 3 floors esp basement 1 and basement 2...a full line of snacks and food for you to choose from. Try coming here for your meals...no regret....I have had enclosed photos of our meals that day...Enjoy!!!!

review_text:
We recently visited Paya Lebar, and it is quickly becoming one of the most convenient and well-developed areas in Singapore for both business and leisure travelers.

The area features modern developments like PLQ Mall and SingPost Centre, offering a great mix of shopping, dining, and everyday essentials. It is clean, well-planned, and less crowded compared to central tourist areas, making it a comfortable location for visitors.

One of the strongest advantages of Paya Lebar is its excellent transport connectivity via Paya Lebar MRT Station, which connects both the East-West and Circle Lines. This makes it extremely easy for travelers to move across Singapore using MRT, taxis, or private transport services.

At HolidaySG, we often assist guests staying in the Paya Lebar area with Singapore attraction tickets and transport services, including city tours, private transfers, and ferry bookings. Its central connectivity allows travelers to easily plan visits to major attractions such as Marina Bay Sands and Gardens by the Bay without any hassle.

We have also coordinated with guests in this area for tours and ticket bookings, ensuring smooth pickup arrangements and seamless travel experiences across Singapore.

Overall, Paya Lebar is a smart and convenient choice for travelers looking for strong connectivity, modern facilities, and easy access to tickets, tours, and transport services in Singapore.

— Team HolidaySG
Singapore Licensed Travel Agency

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Singapore’s MRT stations are exceptionally clean, efficient, and well-organized, reflecting the city’s commitment to seamless public transport. The signage is clear, and announcements are made in multiple languages, making navigation easy for locals and tourists alike. Most stations are air-conditioned and equipped with essential amenities such as escalators, elevators, and ticketing machines. Some central stations also feature retail shops and food stalls, adding convenience for commuters. However, peak hours can get crowded, so traveling during off-peak times is recommended for a more comfortable experience.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Paya Lebar MRT Station is a major interchange in Singapore connecting the **East-West (EW) and Circle (CC) lines**, making it a convenient hub for commuters traveling across the city. The station is modern, spacious, and well-signposted, allowing smooth transfers between lines. Platforms are wide and clean, with ample seating and clear train arrival information. Accessibility is thoughtfully designed, with lifts, escalators, and barrier-free access for wheelchair users and families with strollers. The station is integrated with **Paya Lebar Quarter (PLQ)**, offering direct access to shopping, dining, and office spaces, which adds convenience for both daily commuters and visitors. Peak hours can be crowded, but train frequency helps manage passenger flow efficiently. Overall, Paya Lebar MRT Station is a clean, well-maintained, and highly functional interchange that plays an important role in Singapore’s MRT network, combining connectivity with practical amenities.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: I had a great experience at Bendemeer Market & Food Centre! 🤩 The nasi lemak was truly delicious and satisfying. 😋 The service was efficient, though not exceptional. The atmosphere was adequate, with typical hawker center seating. 🥢 It’s a decent place for a quick meal. 🚶‍♀️

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Ambience is hawker centre so don’t expect aircond etc. But when we are here the weather is quite cooling so it’s okay.
Food is good, drink is good.
Queued about 30min for the prawn noodle, yes it’s good.
The soy milk is good too.
Curry puff also good.
Will come again to also try the dimsum stall which is closed today.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Stumbled upon this gem specialising in beef noodle and soup while exploring this food centre after a long hiatus. The beef tendon soup and grilled beef ribs were impressive - to me, they were pure culinary delight. I am sure I will head back for more.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
The Bendemeer hawker centre has a great variety of food, this review is for a particular stall - Yong Tau Fu.
Selected 5 pieces and add noodles for laksa should be total of 80 cents x 5, add  60 cents, totalling $4.60.
When I asked how much, owner was sneaky and eventually answered $5.40. When eating, I realised he has slipped another piece of brinjal in. Who can finish 2 pcs of chunky brinjal in a bowl?!
To me dishonesty kills it.  Don’t think I’m coming back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Downtown hawker fare next to Boon Keng MRT station.

Bendemeer hawker fare is diverse, affordable, featuring iconic dishes like Chicken Rice, Laksa, Char Kway Teow, Bak Kut Teh, and Kaya Toast, costing just a few dollars per dish and now payable by Grab & Shopee scan & pay functions.

It's a vibrant culinary scene during lunch hours with a hoard of office workers descending onto this hawker center. The queues never lie & those are the stalls offering a taste of authentic popular dishes or they are just economic for daily consumption.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Had Supper here for the very first time. Loved everything about this place. The atmosphere, the music, the food, Chef Badrul and I assume there is another Chef as well, nailed it on the food we ordered, especially since we asked for the pasta to be spicy. And the portions are really generous. Thank you Chef Badrul and Chef.

Shahe was very welcoming from the start, made sure we had all we needed, and giving us chili flakes without any request, was a really nice touch. Also, he saw us taking selfies and offered to help us take photos. Really great service here. Also, as a bartender, the drink, PassionFruit Mango Earl Grey Tea, was off the roof! Thank you Shahe.

Joyce took over, and we ordered a little too much and she was really nice to help pack our food, even suggested separating certain things. Checking on all our food and overall dining experience. Thank you Joyce.

I love the attentiveness of the team. Overall really satisfied. I would definitely come back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Dropped by here for a quick lunch on a weekdays. Still have seats but I still highly recommend to reserve the seats via Google. The food portion is great and we literally can share one course! The weekday lunch is affordable. The roasted garlic is chef kiss. Thank you for this amazing place!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Food was absolutely delicious and flavourful. The seafood aglio olio and Turkey bacon chicken ham pasta was delicious.

Their iced teas were amazing as well with a unique touch and ingredients to it.

The ambience and overall atmosphere of the restaurant was very good and lively. Staffs were very friendly and attentive to our needs.

Overall a very good experience and highly recommended place for family and friends

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This place is worth the hype! Found them through TikTok while searching for a place to celebrate my friend's birthday! The staff are really nice! And were really cute playing the birthday song and providing candles and lighter for the cake!

I recommend their carbonara turkey bacon and tomato vegan pasta! Both dishes surprised me and my friend with their quality!! Not too heavy on the cream for the carbonara, and for the vegan pasta was one of the best vegan version I've tried. For drinks, their premium drinks are really nice especially the strawberry coconut, it has such a smooth feeling and the coconuts really complements the strawberry Earl grey!!

We'll be coming back!!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This is a very pretty cafe near the Bishan Sports Complex. The exterior is decorated with a mini camping feel and the interior is resort-like. You can sit on the camping chairs while waiting for that cup of coffee or food.

Food wise, portions are good for the price paid. We ordered the
. Lamb Chops $24.90
. King Prawns with Tom Yum Pasta $20.90
. Fried Chicken & Waffle $16.90
. Prime Cheese Burger$16.90
. House Salad $9.90
.
The Fried Chicken & Waffle and Tom Yum Pasta were our favourites, especially the pasta. It was a little spicy but the prawns were huge and fresh. The cheeseburger and lamb chops were ok.

The kids ordered the Rainbow Paradise and Salted Caramel milkshake at $9.90 each. They came with a huge scoop of ice cream on top. It is fillling and sweet. I think it is a better idea to share.

This place exudes relaxing vibes so its a good place to chill out over a casual meal.

Weekday Lunch Sets are available too.

place_name: Wordsmith Learning Hub Pte. Ltd. (Main Centre)
review_text:
Hi Team,
Greeting!
Today session was very helpful in improving my understanding of marketing and the tools used to promote it effectively. It was my first time learning how to use these tools, and I gained valuable knowledge on how they can be applied in a practical and effective way.

The second half of the session was especially valuable, as it provided deeper insights into the current challenges faced by the preschool sector. It helped me better understand the industry and its requirements in a timely manner.

The Q&A session was also very beneficial, as it clarified many of my doubts and provided a clear understanding of the future direction and opportunities in this field.

After attending the complete session, I feel much more confident about pursuing a career in teaching. The session has helped remove many of my fears and uncertainties, and I now have a positive outlook towards this profession.

Thank you to the organizers and the entire team for conducting such a useful, informative, and well organized session.                             Thank You!
Parimala Devi

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Wordsmith Learning Hub Pte. Ltd. (Main Centre)
review_text: Thank you for the programme. I found it highly insightful and gained valuable knowledge in marketing strategies. The curriculum was engaging and well-structured, with meaningful sharing sessions that allowed us to learn from one another and broaden our perspectives looking  forward for other  programs

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Wordsmith Learning Hub Pte. Ltd. (Main Centre)
review_text:
Two major sessions were conducted — Marketing and Curriculum. In the Marketing session, I learned various marketing strategies through Facebook and Instagram. I also gained experience in creating logos, posters, videos, and reels using Canva. Canva is a new tool that I learned, and I found it extremely useful. These marketing strategies are not only helpful for home tutoring but can also be applied across different sectors.

I also learned how to create Google Forms and link responses directly to Google Sheets, which are very useful for student inquiries, feedback collection, and enrolment processes.

In the Curriculum session, I learned different teaching methodologies to address the varying abilities of students. This included assessing students in areas such as comprehension, oral skills, and written work, while also focusing on improving their weaker areas. Through this training, I have gained the confidence to teach P1 to P6 students. I also registered for level 2 (Intern). I hope it will increase my confidence level to teach Secondary students.

The session improved my confidence in handling different learning abilities. Useful resources and techniques were shared for better lesson planning. Overall, it was a valuable and enriching learning experience. Thank you Wordsmith.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Wordsmith Learning Hub Pte. Ltd. (Main Centre)
review_text:
Two major sessions were conducted — Marketing and Curriculum. In the Marketing session, I learned various marketing strategies through Facebook and Instagram. I also gained experience in creating logos, posters, videos, and reels using Canva. Canva is a new tool that I learned, and I found it extremely useful. These marketing strategies are not only helpful for home tutoring but can also be applied across different sectors.

I also learned how to create Google Forms and link responses directly to Google Sheets, which are very useful for student inquiries, feedback collection, and enrolment processes.

In the Curriculum session, I learned different teaching methodologies to address the varying abilities of students. This included assessing students in areas such as comprehension, oral skills, and written work, while also focusing on improving their weaker areas. Through this training, I have gained the confidence to teach P1 to P6 students. I also registered for level 2 (Intern). I hope it will increase my confidence level to teach Secondary students.

review_text:
Hi Team,
Greeting!
Today session was very helpful in improving my understanding of marketing and the tools used to promote it effectively. It was my first time learning how to use these tools, and I gained valuable knowledge on how they can be applied in a practical and effective way.

The second half of the session was especially valuable, as it provided deeper insights into the current challenges faced by the preschool sector. It helped me better understand the industry and its requirements in a timely manner.

The Q&A session was also very beneficial, as it clarified many of my doubts and provided a clear understanding of the future direction and opportunities in this field.

After attending the complete session, I feel much more confident about pursuing a career in teaching. The session has helped remove many of my fears and uncertainties, and I now have a positive outlook towards this profession.

Thank you to the organizers and the entire team for conducting such a useful, informative, and well organized session.                             Thank You!
Parimala Devi

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Sengkang MRT/LRT is super convenient — everything you need is connected in one spot. Whether I’m heading to work, grabbing groceries at Compass One, or just getting around the neighborhood, this station makes it easy.

The transfers between MRT and LRT are seamless, and it’s well-integrated with the mall, bus interchange, and even food options nearby. The place is generally clean, organized, and easy to navigate, even during peak hours.

I pass through here almost daily, and I rarely have issues. Trains come on time, announcements are clear, and the whole station feels safe and efficient. One of the better hubs in the northeast, for sure.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

review_text: Was here for an Edusave award ceremony at the MPH, which is clean and has aircon. The open air area is also sheltered and has a huge fan so it's not hot. There are also counters to perform administrative stuff like collection of vouchers etc. There's also a Starbucks here if you wanna chill. There's a 24/7 fitness here too.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
First time booking a pickleball court for play on Tuesday (20 Jan, 8-9am) and when we arrived at the CC, the only hall that we saw was dark and empty. As the counter was not open, I called the main PA hotline and requested for assistance. The staff tried contacting someone from the CC to no avail. He said he would escalate the matter. When I asked if we should just “cancel” our court instead of continuing to wait, he told me to wait for his update. We left eventually and there was no further callback at all.

We returned to the CC at 1pm and checked with the counter staff, only to find out that the pickleball courts are not located within the CC itself, but at the HDB block opposite, on the 2nd floor. When I informed the staff that there’s no notice indicating that the courts are at a different location, the staff replied that their badminton players all know that the courts are opposite. He even asked if I’m staying in the area.

From booking, to the email acknowledgement, there’s no indication of any information stating that. Even at the CC itself, there are no notices giving directions to the courts. Another staff then came out and added that the information on the poster did not say that the courts are in the hall within the CC itself and that the opposite block is part of Bishan CC.

Totally appalled at the attitude of the counter staff, who seemed to imply that it’s our fault for not knowing where the courts are.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
The volunteer staff there were very polite and provided clear, correct guidance. I’m very grateful for their service—it solved my problem and helped me save a considerable amount of money.
Ann Tan

review_text: It’s a cool place to hangout or wait for a friend to meetup. It’s covered so no issues during rain. There is free water refilling, toilet, and Starbucks too if you need coffee. There’s a lot of exercise activities in the morning for seniors, some activities during the day, and cheering squads use it for practice in the evenings. It’s a bustling place and I am glad to be living nearby and can go there anytime.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
This is very great neighbourhood library with librarians who are very observant and go the extra mile. Especially I thank Ms Fazilah 9 Nov 2025 at 7.30pm. She goes the extra mile fuss free, efficient, responsive and responsible. She checked the return conveyor belt and took effort to accompany me downstairs to search shelf and all displays thoroughly. She is very detailed to get both call number and shelf number. She found the book at display within very short time and went back to her routine duty which impresses me with her work ethics.

Before Ms Fazilah helped me I tried to get the book as search shown available on shelf but cannot find. I approached another white hair lady who situated there. She gave up very fast after just finding from the usual numbered shelf which I told her I already done. Then just quickly dismissed me and gave me excuses impatiently that it may be on wrong shelf. This is although I explained I am very sure it should be somewhere in library because not many people in library and I saw no one using the book. So the previous staff did not help me much she did not even look at special display areas...

Thank you so much Sengkang library for having MS Fazilah such a rare, helpful exemplary staff who exhibits great time management and problem solving skill. I thank her for resolving patiently and quickly without excuse and not dismissing customer request.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We recently took our little one to Sengkang Public Library, which is conveniently located right inside Compass One. It made for a really easy family outing, as we could grab a quick bite at the mall before heading up. The library itself is quite spacious and felt very clean and well-maintained, spread across two levels. Our child absolutely loved the Early Literacy Area; they have some really engaging displays and the book modules, designed like traditional fishing baskets, were a nice touch that caught our attention. It’s a bright and welcoming space, perfect for little hands to explore.

While the children's section can get a bit lively, as expected with so many excited kids, there are plenty of other quiet spots throughout the library if you need a calmer environment. My wife and I appreciated the wide selection of books for all ages, and I even spotted a dedicated 'Tween Space' which will be great for our child in a few years. There are ample seating areas, so it's easy to settle down with a stack of books and spend a good hour or two. It’s definitely a solid choice for a family afternoon out, combining learning with comfort.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

The first thing I noticed that drew my attention when I walked in was this little Harry Potter castle, which was on top of a bookshelf. The second thing I noticed was that they had a huge selection of audiovisuals.

Once you walk a bit more towards the left, you’ll see a few bookshelves, and close to those bookshelves is a long white table. And near the white table there are 2 seats close to a window. I think that area is a really nice spot for reading, studying, and using your computers.

If you want straight, you’ll see comics, and a teen section. In between 2 bookshelves, there are 2 seats, for safe distancing. There is also a magazine section.

There is also a quick charge area. Near the quick charge area there are a few shelves of adult fiction.

Overall, I think the Sengkang Library is a nice library for spending a long duration of time with your computer, or having a book you want to read. You can also study in the area of the white table and 2 seats near the window.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: This is a two storey library and that easily makes it better. If you feel like the children section can be loud, you may find other reading spots. Conveniently located inside the mall with lots of reading books for small children. Lots of library staff too to remind the children to keep quiet while inside the library. My daughters love to hang out in this library after school.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Located within Compass One shopping mall, the library covers an area of 2,136 square meters, New areas in the library include the Early Literacy Area where families can read and bond together, the Tween Space targeted at children aged 10 to 14, and a dedicated Adults section for quiet reading.  It was officially opened on 30 November 2002, comprising of 2 levels - it is convenient and accessible in the shopping centre.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: This hawker has many good stalls and the place is airy enough. Our favourite is Zuzu, a Turkish stall whose butter rice and kebabs are excellent. Apparently they have two restaurants in Arab Street by the same name. The nasi lemak stall is also good with reasonable price but note that they tend to close anytime they like. Normally, they are closed on Fridays and Mondays. There are two soya bean stalls here, the lesser known one is cheaper. There is a stall selling home made fish cake which is very delicious. And the teochew porridge stall has a wide selection of home cooked dishes.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: This place car park is crowded even though there are seats in hawker centre. The exit out to main serangoon garden way is always jam. Other than transport, there are crowd in nasi lemak stall, prawn noodle, pork soup and bread stall. Not always have all kinds of local delights.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I would give 10 stars if it is possible. I highly recommend trying the gong bao ji from the zi char store. The portion was generous, the caramelized onions complemented the chicken. It is as if the hawkers go through cryptobiosis on non opening hours to preserve their adenosine triphosphate energy solely for cooking. The price is also reasonable, not cheap nor expensive.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Seng Kee Bak Chor Mee is a must-try — the noodles are springy, the mushroom sauce paired with the chilli-vinegar sauce is flavourful with just the right kick, and the rich, comforting broth ties everything together perfectly. Easily one of my favourite bak chor mee stalls in Singapore. You can even check if their latest happenings on their Instagram @sengkeebcm

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
NEX is a big, convenient mall that’s hard to beat for errands or a casual day out. It’s right by Serangoon MRT and the bus interchange, so getting in and out is easy. Inside, it’s bright, clean, and well-air-conditioned; even on weekends when it’s busy, the walkways are wide enough that you can still move comfortably.

Food options are plentiful, everything from quick bites to sit-down spots, and the food court is efficient with staff constantly wiping tables, so turnover is fast. Shopping is just as varied, with fashion, beauty, tech, and a large supermarket, which makes it great for one-stop “get everything done” runs. Families will appreciate the clear signage and clean restrooms, plus plenty of places to take a break if you’re with kids or older folks. Overall, a reliable place to eat, shop, and cool off, practical for a lunch stop and pleasant enough for an unhurried weekend stroll.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Huge shopping mall connected to Serangoon MRT. Has all outlets that you need for shopping and food.
Very accessible. Can get a bit crowded on weekends.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Probably the busiest, most bustling mall in the heartlands. It’s a decent size with a pretty good tenant mix, but word on the street is that the old Isetan spot is being turned into a bunch of boring tuition centres and a Kiztopia. If that’s true, we’ll probably be visiting a lot less.

There’s also been a massive proliferation of PRC restaurants in recent years, which makes us even less inclined to frequent the place. We might drop by when Chin Mee Chin finally opens, but we aren’t going to visit as much as before.

P.S The elevators are always under maintenance or not working.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The management of NEX mall (Serangoon MRT) must be fast asleep. A couple of their escalators (to L2 & B1, see photos) have been out of service for quite a few months and no repair work has been initiated. These escalators are covered with cladding and nobody has been spotted working on them! Rather than have them fixed, signs are instead posted to direct customers to other (working, for the time being) escalators. This smacks of apathy and a pathetic lack of professional pride & customer care. Whoever is at the top needs to deal with it - unless, of course, he/she/they also can’t be bothered. In which case, the problem is endemic.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
NEX is a big, convenient mall that’s hard to beat for errands or a casual day out. It’s right by Serangoon MRT and the bus interchange, so getting in and out is easy. Inside, it’s bright, clean, and well-air-conditioned; even on weekends when it’s busy, the walkways are wide enough that you can still move comfortably.

Food options are plentiful, everything from quick bites to sit-down spots, and the food court is efficient with staff constantly wiping tables, so turnover is fast. Shopping is just as varied, with fashion, beauty, tech, and a large supermarket, which makes it great for one-stop “get everything done” runs. Families will appreciate the clear signage and clean restrooms, plus plenty of places to take a break if you’re with kids or older folks. Overall, a reliable place to eat, shop, and cool off, practical for a lunch stop and pleasant enough for an unhurried weekend stroll.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Huge shopping mall connected to Serangoon MRT. Has all outlets that you need for shopping and food.
Very accessible. Can get a bit crowded on weekends.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Probably the busiest, most bustling mall in the heartlands. It’s a decent size with a pretty good tenant mix, but word on the street is that the old Isetan spot is being turned into a bunch of boring tuition centres and a Kiztopia. If that’s true, we’ll probably be visiting a lot less.

There’s also been a massive proliferation of PRC restaurants in recent years, which makes us even less inclined to frequent the place. We might drop by when Chin Mee Chin finally opens, but we aren’t going to visit as much as before.

P.S The elevators are always under maintenance or not working.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Good Location Shopping!!!
But saw such behaviours of youngsters is totally embarrassing and flabergusted, giving others no place to walk via bulldozing in a row.
Saw them few times doing this in 2025 and 2 times in 2026. Video was captured when I changed my handphone and was trying out it's functions.
The worst is, this group have been doing this bulldozing walks everywhere they go, even Tourists has been complaining on TikTok regarding such behaviours when they visited Singapore.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It's a beautiful place we can see Singapore and Malaysia at that same time. When I went it's full rainy and I took photoshoot there. We want to spend peace area I will suggest The Woodlands Waterfront. Beautiful silent area. We will hear only Air sound, Water sound.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Here's a review you can use:

---

Ryan chou
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Woodlands Waterfront Park is one of my favorite spots to unwind. The park offers a beautiful, relaxing view of the Johor Strait, especially during sunset. The waterfront promenade is perfect for a peaceful stroll or a bike ride, and there’s plenty of open space for picnics with family and friends.

I also love the iconic jetty—it’s a great place to take in the sea breeze and watch the boats go by. The park is well-maintained, with clean paths and plenty of benches to sit and enjoy the scenery. Highly recommended for anyone looking to escape the city hustle without going too far!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It is a great place to be with friends. We can enjoy the grennery and see the sea view of malaysua from here. It is quite hot in the afternoon so pls plan your trip here in the morning where it is not so hot. There is some bus stop near here but you can also walk here from woodlands mrt throguh Admiralty park. Not many facilities here except for the seafood restaurant and just greenery around.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 5-Star Review for Wildseed @ The Summerhouse

Wah, come to Wildseed Cafe at The Summerhouse and kena first-class service sia! Staff got eagle eyes one — see us trying to 自拍 (take photo) until hand stretch like yoga pose, immediately volunteer to help take nice photos. End result so good, can use for profile picture already!

Our order taking a bit longer, but the staff never disappear like ninja. They proactively checked with the kitchen and kept us updated. Service level really power lah.

Vegetarian choices got plenty plenty, not the usual “salad only” situation. The Truffled Brie Pizza was the superstar of the day. So fragrant and cheesy that every slice disappeared faster than salary after payday. The truffle aroma attack the nose first, then the creamy brie come in for the finishing move.

Good food, beautiful surroundings, and service that makes you feel welcomed. Come hungry, leave happy, and maybe with a few new photos for social media too! 😆🍕🌿📸

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Lovely staff who were friendly and patient even on a hot afternoon. The first pizza came slightly burnt but the staff offered to redo it which we were very thankful for. Wings were quite tasty too! Not recommended to go there on a hot afternoon though as it is an open-air cafe.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
The food was quite standard, nothing particularly special or memorable. Considering the price, I personally don’t think it offers great value. The environment and dining experience were also fairly ordinary.

However, if you would like to experience the unique black-and-white house surroundings and atmosphere, it may still be worth visiting once.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Called prior to arrival to request for indoor seating due to hot weather. Given a nice table within the air-conditioned place but it was not enough to cool us down. Felt a little stuffy and our group felt warm throughout the meal.

Came on a weekday afternoon and place was almost empty.

We were presented with the lunch set menu just 2 min before promotion was over (2.30pm). We tried to order the set online at 2.35pm and was only then informed it wasn't available! We were disappointed since we thought having been presented the menu by the staff, we would be able to order the set, which is a good deal (1 drink + 1 side+ 1 main for 29.90 while usually a normal main dish is already ~32). Tried reasoning with the staff to no avail.

After we started placing our normal orders and submitting the orders, someone came and told us the manager agreed to let us order the set menu. Poor management on this part, in my opinion.

Food came nicely presented and taste legit. Mushroom soup was thick. Steak was nicely done to our request. Coffee of good size. Desserts need wait but was served hot.

Overall ambience a nice place to hang out for a couple of drinks and tea.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Beautiful restaurant, with all the warm light bulbs and live band, great atmosphere to dine in! But food was just disappointing.🥲

Soft shell crab linguine: rate 3/5 stars
- linguine not al dente.
- smallest soft shell crab i have ever eaten
- not sure what is the purpose of putting a whole chili intact on the pasta instead of chopping it. We just took it out and place it aside. The pasta wasn't even spicy at all, so i guess they expect customer to bite on that chili directly while having the pasta?
- significantly overpriced. $29 for that little bowl of pasta.

Sweet potato fries $14 (rate 4/5 stars)
- love it, but it isnt crispy at all. Its texture is rather soft n moist to me.

Sunset fizz mocktail $10 (rate 1/5 stars)
- nothing special. It tastes like the sunkist drink that u have on a plane.

Overall experience: Not worth the price. Not worth the visit.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Facilities:
The Olympic-sized swimming pool is a highlight, with plenty of space for both casual swimmers and classes. The water playground is perfect for young kids to splash around, while parents can relax in the jacuzzi. Do note that some lanes are reserved for lessons, usually in the mornings or late afternoons, but it’s a small trade-off for such excellent facilities. Swimming classes are reasonably priced at ~$100/month (4 lessons), though kids need to pass a water confidence test to join.

Kid-Friendly Features:
The kiddie playroom is a lifesaver! Stocked with Fisher-Price toys, it’s a cozy spot where kids can play while parents enjoy a meal at the nearby alfresco area. The glass walls allow for easy supervision, and it’s wonderful to see kids mingling with new friends. A small but important note: kids over 1.2m aren’t allowed in the playroom, so it’s ideal for younger children.

Dining:
Food options cater to all tastes and occasions. The Atrium Cafe offers casual dining with a mix of Western and local dishes, making it perfect for a quick bite. For something more upscale, the Garden Grill on the second floor is a must-try. With a changing monthly menu inspired by different countries, it keeps things exciting. While not every dish is a hit, the freshly baked bread rolls and butter are consistently amazing. The colonial-style setting adds a touch of charm, though note the dress code.

Fitness & Activities:
Fitness enthusiasts will love the gym overlooking the pool, recently renovated for a fresh and modern feel. The club also boasts tennis courts, a bowling alley, and squash courts, so there’s plenty to keep everyone active. The adjacent studio hosts classes like yoga and Zumba, making it convenient for parents to work out while the kids are engaged in their own activities.

Convenience:
Parking is a breeze, and its location in Serangoon Gardens means you’re just minutes away from other eateries and amenities.

Overall, Serangoon Country Club is a well-rounded spot for families, combining excellent facilities with thoughtful touches for parents and kids alike.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Service staff Lelia stationed at the bowling alley was very rude, condescending and was not willing to listen to our explanation after an accident occurred. She was speaking in a way that made it seem like I had willingly and willfully caused that accident on purpose. After she had repeatedly cut me off and choose not to listen to me as I tried to explain the situation, she began speaking to me and my friends in a very condescending and unacceptable tone. I accept responsibility for the accident but I still believe that her tone and the was she carried herself was very unacceptable and could be better. However the same cannot be said about the rest of the club and it's staff as they were very welcoming and accommodating.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: This is a Very established family club with many amenities and facilities. On the heliconia Wing there is a huge function room on the 2nd level. The first level features a Bowling alley and a Chinese restaurant. In the main wing there are several food and beverages options. A causual dining on the first floor, formal dining on the 2nd floor, a night lounge with live band, poolside cafe serving ice cream and other finger food and bbq area.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Attended wedding reception of my dear friends daughter. Beautiful hall. Well lit and loved the deco. Food was simply delicious

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: I like the newly renovated gym, which comes with 2 storey, the upper one overlooking the swimming pool. The lounge with live band is a very good place to chill, and has darts corner. Will be better if they put a new pool table and make it like previous sports bar. The atrium food standard has dropped over the years, and noticed a lot of the staff has changed too. Overall a nice place to hang out with family friends or clients.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Chomp Chomp was established in 1972 as a "hawkers centre", now better known as foodcentre. It has been upgraded a few times over the years, the latest one was end of 2025 till March 2026. Our cleaning manager gave us the tip about the great food at the very recently reopened foodhall. And she was spot on, what an absolute treat, this hidden gem. A must visit, higly recommended.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Favourite hawker centre !! I have been a regular diner at Chomp Chomp since I was young. The standard still feels around the same. I usually get the sambal stingray, oyster omelette, satay, bbq wings and carrot cake. The seats inside the centre are sometimes stuffy but the ones outside and at the back are really nice. love love love

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Lots of local dishes to choose. After renovation the place is still SMALL. Come early if you like a good seat. Better ventilation with the huge fans. Some of the stalls are no longer there.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
The XXL sugarcane juice is the hit.
Other foods are average, some of them require fairly long waiting time.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Closed from 27 October 2025 to 26 January 2026 for renovations and upgrading works. This is a great food centre with a diverse selection of hawker food. Been eating here when i was younger and living in Serangoon Gardens. My current fav char kway teow is located at this food centre and they have quite a few good stalls serving BBQ sambal stingray.

On my last visit I was surprised to see that Chomp Chomp isn't as crowded as it used to be back in the day. On the bright side, it’s way easier to get a seat now.
